CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-02-2017 Assigned To Arbitration On Jul-17-17

Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar May-03-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MONICA NEMEC, TEL. NO. 1-510-703-5000 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. RENEE B. BEA OF THE SINGER/BEA LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-500-6079 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Amir Mozaffarian's demurrer to all four causes of action in the first amended complaint is overruled as to the first cause of action for violation of Civil Code 1749 and the fourth cause of action for violation of the Unfair Competition Law and sustained without leave to amend as to the second cause of action for violation of Civil Code 1723 and the third cause of action for violation of the Consumer Legal Remedies Act. There is nothing necessarily inconsistent between the allegations of the original complaint and the first amended complaint, so the sham pleading doctrine does not bar any of plaintiff John Nemec's claims. Mr. Nemec adequately alleges that the transaction is a layaway agreement form for purposes of section 1749. A fair reading of section 1749 provides that violation of that section permits a party such as Mr. Nemec to rescind the transaction. Liberally construed, the first amended complaint seeks rescissionary damages (i.e. damages in the amount of the deposits made by Mr. Nemec). Mr. Nemec's section 1723 claim fails as a matter of law because exhibit A to the first amended complaint conspicuously states Mr. Mozaffarian's refund policy and that exhibit is an order form for purposes of section 1723. Mr. Nemec's CLRA claim fails as a matter of law because his section 1723 claim is not viable and there is nothing unconscionable about Mr. Mozaffarian's refund policy. Mr. Nemec adequately alleged a UCL claim per the unlawful prong based on a violation of section 1749. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/hk
